topic.what: Interactive platform for systematic evaluation of machine-generated text (MGT) detectors
topic.how: Unified end-to-end pipeline with Dataset Building, Dataset Attack, Detector Training, and Performance Evaluation components supporting 26 detectors and 12 attack methods
topic.why_matters: Existing MGT detector evaluations are fragmented across datasets, preprocessing, attacks, and metrics; MGTEVAL provides reproducible, configurable, fair comparison under consistent protocols

characteristics.evidence: Longformer achieved best accuracy in standardized evaluation
characteristics.context: Binary detection (human vs machine) on SemEval 2024 + Qwen3-4B dataset

characteristics.what_it_measures: Harmonic mean of precision and recall for machine-generated class

characteristics.what_itasures: Area under ROC curve for ranking quality

characteristics.what_it_measures: True positive rate at 0.1% false positive rate
characteristics.vs_baseline: strict operating point for real-world deployment
characteristics.evidence: Longformer maintains high TPR under stringent FPR constraint

characteristics.vs_baseline: PECoLA fastest at 6.86ms, RADAR slowest at 2067ms
characteristics.evidence: Range spans 3 orders of magnitude across detector families
characteristics.context: Efficiency vs effectiveness tradeoff varies widely

characteristics.what_it_measures: Peak GPU memory consumption during evaluation
characteristics.evidence: Memory footprint varies by detector architecture
characteristics.context: Deployment feasibility depends on available hardware

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of originally-correct classifications flipped by attacks
Samples processed per second during evaluation

good_for: rapid prototyping of new detection methods
requires: Standardized dataset format or custom corpus
Computational resources for full pipeline execution

novelty: First integrated end-to-end platform unifying dataset construction, attack generation, detector training, and evaluation
Registry-based modular design enabling seamless extension of detectors and attacks
First simultaneous benchmark of 26 MGT detectors with 12 attack methods
Dual CLI and WebUI interfaces for varied expertise levels
Calibration framework for metric-based detectors via logistic regression mapping scores to probabilities
open_problems: Computational expense of full pipeline limits usability in resource-constrained environments
Detector-specific hyperparameter fidelity not fully preserved in unified protocol (leads to discrepancies from original papers)
Currently limited to binary detection; lacks fine-grained source attribution
Multilingual and cross-domain benchmarking not yet supported
Richer attack compositions and adaptive attack-defense evaluation not covered
Broader detector families (LLM-based, API-based) need integration
